# R1.05 · Cache stampede prevention

> When a hot key expires under heavy concurrency, every reader misses at once and hammers the source. EchoCache stops
> the herd by construction: a single-flight `flights` map (one fill per herd) and a jittered `expires_at` TTL so a
> cohort never expires together. The lock-on-miss `SET … NX PX` pattern is taught as the generic contrast. Served at
> /redis-patterns/caching/cache-stampede-prevention.

## Pages

- [Hub](/redis-patterns/caching/cache-stampede-prevention): the herd problem and how EchoCache prevents it.
- [Lock-on-miss](/redis-patterns/caching/cache-stampede-prevention/lock-on-miss): the generic per-key regeneration lock (`SET … NX PX`) and its token-checked release — the contrast to EchoCache's in-process single-flight.
- [Early refresh](/redis-patterns/caching/cache-stampede-prevention/early-refresh): X-Fetch as the pattern; EchoCache's real mechanism is expiry jitter (`ttl ± ttl·jitter`), not probabilistic recompute.
- [Request coalescing](/redis-patterns/caching/cache-stampede-prevention/coalescing): concurrent misses collapse onto one in-flight load via the `flights` map and the `:coalesced` counter — one fill, every waiter served.

## Up

- [R1 · Caching](/redis-patterns/caching) · [Course home](/redis-patterns)
